title: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (UIP pattern)
vignette: A 62-year-old man comes to the clinic due to a 6-month history of progressive exertional dyspnea. The patient has an occasional cough but has no palpitations, orthopnea, chest pain, or lower extremity swelling. Past medical history is insignificant. He works as an attorney, does not use tobacco, and drinks alcohol only on social occasions. His symptoms continue to progress, and the patient dies of respiratory failure 3 years after the initial clinic visit. Autopsy findings include heterogenous lung parenchyma with predominantly subpleural areas of dense collagen deposition, lymphocyte infiltration, and fibroblast proliferation intermixed with areas of normal lung tissue. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

clues.note: Insidious, progressive exertional dyspnea over months with a dry cough in an older adult is the classic presentation of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF).

clues.text: works as an attorney, does not use tobacco
clues.note: No occupational dust/asbestos exposure and no smoking — this argues AGAINST pneumoconioses (asbestosis) and smoking-related disease, supporting an IDIOPATHIC fibrosis.

clues.text: predominantly subpleural areas of dense collagen deposition
clues.note: Subpleural, patchy fibrosis is the distribution of usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP), the histologic pattern of IPF. Subpleural/basal predominance is characteristic.

clues.text: fibroblast proliferation intermixed with areas of normal lung tissue
clues.note: Fibroblastic foci with TEMPORAL and spatial heterogeneity (fibrosis next to normal lung) defines the UIP pattern of IPF. The patchwork of old fibrosis, active fibroblast foci, and normal lung is diagnostic.

whatMatters: Older man, progressive dyspnea, NO exposures, dying of respiratory failure, with subpleural patchy fibrosis + fibroblastic foci + temporal heterogeneity (UIP pattern) = idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The 'no asbestos exposure' and the heterogeneous subpleural UIP histology rule out asbestosis and the other options.
strategy: For fibrotic ILD, read the histology pattern and the exposure history together. UIP pattern (subpleural, patchy, fibroblastic foci, temporal heterogeneity) WITHOUT an identifiable cause = IPF. An exposure (asbestos bodies, antigens, drugs) would redirect you to a secondary cause.

eliminations.label: alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency — emphysema, not fibrosis
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency causes panacinar EMPHYSEMA (and liver disease), typically in younger patients. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it destroys alveolar walls (obstructive), not subpleural fibrosis with fibroblastic foci. ✅ Correct if: histology showed alveolar-wall destruction/emphysema in a young nonsmoker with liver disease.

eliminations.label: asbestosis — needs exposure + asbestos bodies (your pick)
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: asbestosis is fibrosis from asbestos exposure (shipbuilding, insulation), with ferruginous/asbestos bodies and pleural plaques. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: this attorney has NO asbestos exposure and the autopsy shows no asbestos bodies/pleural plaques — UIP without exposure is IPF, not asbestosis. ✅ Correct if: there were an occupational asbestos history with asbestos bodies and pleural plaques.

eliminations.label: chronic bronchitis — productive cough, not fibrosis
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: chronic bronchitis is defined clinically by a productive cough >=3 months in 2 years, with mucous gland hyperplasia. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it's an obstructive airway disease (usually smokers), not subpleural interstitial fibrosis. ✅ Correct if: there were chronic productive cough with Reid index increase in a smoker.

eliminations.label: chronic hypersensitivity pneumonitis — needs antigen exposure
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: hypersensitivity pneumonitis is immune-mediated lung inflammation/fibrosis from inhaled antigens (molds, birds), often with peribronchiolar, upper-lobe changes and granulomas. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: there's no antigen exposure, and the pattern here is subpleural UIP without granulomas — favoring IPF. ✅ Correct if: there were a clear antigen exposure (bird/mold) with poorly formed granulomas.

eliminations.label: cryptogenic organizing pneumonia — different histology/course
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: COP shows intra-alveolar fibroblastic plugs (Masson bodies) and typically responds to steroids. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: the autopsy shows progressive subpleural dense fibrosis with fibroblastic foci and temporal heterogeneity (UIP), not organizing-pneumonia plugs; the relentless fatal course fits IPF. ✅ Correct if: histology showed organizing pneumonia (Masson bodies) with a subacute, steroid-responsive course.

reasoning: Older adult, progressive dyspnea, NO exposures, fatal course
Histology: subpleural patchy fibrosis + fibroblastic foci + temporal heterogeneity = UIP
UIP without identifiable cause = idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
mechanism: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis is a chronic, progressive fibrosing interstitial pneumonia of unknown cause (risk: age, smoking, genetics like telomerase mutations) defined by the usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern. Repetitive alveolar epithelial injury triggers aberrant fibroblast/myofibroblast proliferation (fibroblastic foci), producing patchy subpleural and basal fibrosis with honeycombing and temporal/spatial heterogeneity. There is no curative therapy and prognosis is poor; antifibrotics (pirfenidone, nintedanib) slow decline.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Pulmonary > restrictive/interstitial disease: IPF (UIP pattern — subpleural/basal, fibroblastic foci, temporal heterogeneity, honeycombing), risk factors, antifibrotics. Contrast asbestosis, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, COP.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: Combine 'no exposure' + the UIP histology to land on IDIOPATHIC fibrosis. The temporal heterogeneity and subpleural distribution are the diagnostic hooks.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on UIP, fibroblastic foci, temporal/spatial heterogeneity, subpleural, honeycombing, asbestos bodies? Tell me which and I'll explain.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Asbestosis is the main trap because both cause fibrosis — but asbestosis needs EXPOSURE + asbestos bodies/pleural plaques; this attorney has neither, so UIP without cause = IPF.

difficulty.label: 🖼️ Imaging / diagram
difficulty.message: Histology/imaging recognition: UIP shows subpleural/basal patchy fibrosis, fibroblastic foci, and honeycombing on CT. Asbestosis adds pleural plaques and asbestos bodies — look for those to differentiate.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Tell me what happened (timing, second-guessing, careless click) and I'll suggest a fix for future questions.

keywords.back: Subpleural/basal patchy fibrosis, fibroblastic foci, temporal heterogeneity, honeycombing
Asbestos exposure, ferruginous/asbestos bodies, pleural plaques
Productive cough >=3 months/yr x2 yrs; Reid index up

firstAid.detail: Pulmonary > restrictive ILD: IPF/UIP pattern (subpleural fibrosis, fibroblastic foci, honeycombing), risk factors, antifibrotic therapy, prognosis.
firstAid.location: Pulmonary > restrictive ILD: IPF/UIP pattern (subpleural fibrosis, fibroblastic foci, honeycombing), risk factors, antifibrotic therapy, prognosis.

firstAid.detail: Pulmonary > asbestosis (and pneumoconioses), hypersensitivity pneumonitis, cryptogenic organizing pneumonia, chronic bronchitis, alpha-1 antitrypsin emphysema.
firstAid.location: Pulmonary > asbestosis (and pneumoconioses), hypersensitivity pneumonitis, cryptogenic organizing pneumonia, chronic bronchitis, alpha-1 antitrypsin emphysema.

firstAid.detail: Pulmonary > restrictive PFTs (low FVC, high FEV1/FVC, low DLCO) tying histology to function.
firstAid.location: Pulmonary > restrictive PFTs (low FVC, high FEV1/FVC, low DLCO) tying histology to function.

memoryHook: Old lungs, no exposures, patchy SUBPLEURAL fibrosis with fibroblastic foci of different ages = IPF (UIP). Add asbestos bodies/plaques → asbestosis instead.
